creator: taosecurity@gmail.com
text:
FreeBSD 7.0-RC1 doesn't detect the hard drive installed in a Dell PowerEdge 750.  FreeBSD 7.0-BETA4 does.  This is dmesg from 7.0-BETA4 which detects the HDD properly.

Trying to mount root from ufs:/dev/ad2s1a
em0: link state changed to UP

Fix: 

I suspect changes to ata-chipset.c between 7.0-BETA4 and 7.0-RC1 may be the culprit but I do not have the technical knowledge to be sure.

ata-chipset.c
Revision 1.202.2.5
Sun Dec 9 19:26:56 2007 UTC (3 weeks, 3 days ago) by delphij

Changes since revision 1.202.2.4: +131 -41 lines

Sync up ata(4) work by sos@ on -HEAD:

 * Add preliminary SATA ATAPI support for sii 3132/3124 chips.
 * Fix speed report on Intel SATA chips in compat mode.
 * Fix broken detect of JMicron 368.

Related revisions:
 - ata-chipset.c: 1.208, 1.210
 - ata-disk.c.c:  1.205

Approved by:	re (kensmith), sos

http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/cvsweb.cgi/src/sys/dev/ata/?sortby=date;only_with_tag=RELENG_7_0#dirlist

Thank you for looking at this!
How-To-Repeat: Try to install 7.0-RC1.  It will not detect the HDD.

Originally submitted to freebsd-current as

http://unix.derkeiler.com/Mailing-Lists/FreeBSD/current/2008-01/msg00029.html
